title: Dodz
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}} {{CelestialBody |image=[[File:Dodz-SWDroids1986-1.jpg]] |name=Dodz |region=*[[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for [[Dodz system/Legends|Dodz system]] *[[The Slice/Legends|The Slice]]{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} places the [[Dodz system/Legends|Dodz system]], and thereby Dodz itself, in the [[Zoraster sector/Legends|Zoraster sector]], which {{SW|url=news/essential-atlas-outer-rim-sectors|text=The Essential Atlas Extra: Outer Rim Sectors|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} depicts as part of what ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' identifies as [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]]. |sector=[[Zoraster sector/Legends|Zoraster sector]] |system=[[Dodz system/Legends|Dodz system]] |coordinates=U-13''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' — Based on corresponding data for [[Dodz system/Legends|Dodz system]] |xyz= |distance= |suns= |orbited= |position= |moons= |routes= |lengthday= |lengthyear= |class=Terrestrial[[Droids (1986) 1|''Droids'' (1986) 1]] |diameter= |atmosphere= |climate= |gravity= |terrain=[[Stone/Legends|Rocky]] cliffs |water= |interest=*[[Council Dwelling]] *[[Crystal mines]] *[[Dwelling of Lott Kemp]] *[[Jost Ellon's cave]] *[[Mansion of Governor Kugg]] |flora= |fauna= |otherlife= |species= |otherspecies=[[Human/Legends|Humans]] |socialgroup= |language=[[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]] |government= |population= |demonym= |cities= |imports= |exports= |affiliation= }} {{Quote|And the only way you can ''thank'' me is to remember what that silent figure of the ''Ranger'' means to all of us on this planet!|Jost Ellon, to Althon|}} '''Dodz''' was a small, barren [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Zoraster sector/Legends|Zoraster sector]]. It featured extensive [[Crystal/Legends|crystal]] [[Cave/Legends|caverns]], and at some point a profitable crystal [[Mining/Legends|mining]] industry developed on the planet, with the local community being governed by the [[Citizen's Council of Dodz]]. However, power on Dodz was eventually usurped by the tyrannical [[Governor/Legends|Governor]] [[Kugg]], who used the [[Battle droid/Legends|combat droid]] [[Destroyer (droid)|Destroyer]] to coerce the local [[Merchant/Legends|merchants]] into paying heavy taxes. Around [[15 BBY/Legends|15 BBY]], the [[Droid/Legends|droids]] [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]], who had been acquired by the merchant [[Lott Kemp]] on Dodz, arrived on the planet, only to discover that their new owner had been driven off-world by Kugg and the Destroyer. Instead, they met the young [[Scavenger/Legends|scavenger]] [[Jost Ellon]], who invited them to the [[Jost Ellon's cave|cave]] where he lived. There, the trio re-activated what was, unbeknown to Ellon, an old [[Unidentified Ranger X-1|Ranger X-1]] [[Bodyguard droid/Legends|defense droid]]. Hopeful that the [[Ranger X-1|droid]] could be used to fight back against Kugg's tyranny, Ellon took the droids to a meeting of the local Citizen's Council of Dodz, albeit missing the appearance of Kugg himself there. On the following [[Local day/Legends|day]], however, Ellon revealed himself and the Ranger X-1 to the governor, and the Destroyer was ordered to attack the other automaton. The two droids engaged in a brawl, with interference by R2-D2 ultimately allowing the X-1 to blow up the Destroyer at the cost of all of his remaining energy. The Citizen's Council of Dodz subsequently intended to hand Kugg over to an [[intergalactic trial]], while an [[Intergalactic Droid Agency shuttle]] later took C-3PO and R2-D2 offworld due to the latter having sustained critical damage during the battle between the X-1 and the Destroyer that could not be repaired on Dodz. ==Description== {{Quote|''Dodz''? It's so ''tiny'' it only has ''half'' a moon! Ha ha!''"
"''And even ''that'' doesn't make its barren land any more appealing! Hah, hah ek!|Two droids|}} Dodz was a small{{CSWECite|I|194|Dodz}} [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Dodz system/Legends|Dodz system]], a part of the [[Zoraster sector/Legends|Zoraster sector]] in [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. It had a pockmarked, barren surface that appeared [[Color/Legends|gray]] from [[Realspace/Legends|space]], and at least one area of the planet featured [[Stone/Legends|rocky]] cliffs. Dodz was known for its extensive [[Crystal/Legends|crystal]] [[Cave/Legends|caverns]].{{TORcite|exp=shadow|type=crewskill|Archaeology|Small Planet, Big Caves}} ==History== ===Tyranny on a crystal world=== {{Quote|Our fathers fought hard against evil men like ''Kugg'' to free this land!|An old man on Dodz|}} {{Gamemechanics|Star Wars: The Old Republic: Shadow of Revan}} Around [[3637 BBY]],{{TORweb|url=community/showthread.php?p=9852711#edit9852711|text=Charles Boyd on Forums: "Dear Story Team, What Year Are We Currently In?" (1)}} — According to ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]''{{'s}} lead designer, [[Charles Boyd]], the majority of the events of the digital expansion [[Star Wars: The Old Republic: Shadow of Revan|''Shadow of Revan'']] take place in approximately [[3637 BBY]]. The Crew Skill mission "Small Planet, Big Caves" is part of the ''Shadow of Revan'' expansion and therefore also takes place around that [[Standard year/Legends|year]]. an individual affiliated with one of the sides in the ongoing [[Sith Wars/Legends|struggle]] between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|reconstituted Sith Empire]] sent a companion to harvest some crystals from the purported crystal caverns on Dodz. {{Endgame}} [[File:JostEllonRangerX1-SWDroids1986-1.jpg|thumb|left|250px|A deactivated Ranger X-1 came to rest near the crystal mines on Dodz.]] At some point, Dodz was [[Colonist/Legends|settled]], with [[crystal mine]]s being set up on the planet. A [[Unidentified Ranger X-1|Ranger X-1]] [[Bodyguard droid/Legends|defense droid]] eventually came to rest in a deactivated state near the [[Mining/Legends|mines]]. The [[3PO-series protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] later speculated that the [[Ranger X-1|unit]] had been brought to the planet by crystal [[Miner/Legends|miners]] for protection and that he had subsequently been overlooked by the [[Intergalactic Law Agency]]—formed during the final [[Standard century/Legends|century]] of the existence of the Republic{{CSWECite|II|107|Intergalactic Law Agency}}—when it was collecting and scrapping any remaining Ranger X-1s after it had terminated the series. According to a [[Unidentified Human male (Dodz)|member]] of the Dodz colony, its population at some point was also engaged in a bitter struggle against tyrannical parties to liberate their territories. Much later, the [[Alien/Legends|alien]] [[Kugg]] usurped the [[Politics/Legends|political]] power on Dodz, ruling it for a long time as a tyrannical [[Governor/Legends|governor]]. He imposed heavy taxes on the planet's [[Merchant/Legends|merchants]], and in the case of their refusal to pay them, dispatched the [[Battle droid/Legends|combat droid]] [[Destroyer (droid)|Destroyer]] to wreck their property or even [[Death/Legends|kill]] them. Kugg also murdered{{SW|url=news/the-droids-re-animated-part-1|series=The Droids Re-Animated|text=Part 1|nolive=1}} the [[Jost Ellon's family|family]] of the young [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Jost Ellon]]. The youth eventually became a [[Scavenger/Legends|scavenger]] who lived in a [[Jost Ellon's cave|cave]] and at some point retrieved the deactivated X-1, albeit without realizing its true nature as a [[Droid/Legends|droid]]. ===New arrivals=== {{Quote|I just had to make sure you didn't work for ''Kugg''!''"
"''Kugg, sir?''"
"''Yeah, Kugg, the so-called ''governor'' of this planet! ''[…]'' If the merchants here refuse to pay Kugg's heavy taxes, he sends his ''Destroyer''… a ''killer droid''… to deal with them!|Jost Ellon and C-3PO|}} [[File:Dwelling_of_Lott_Kemp.jpg|thumb|right|300px|C-3PO and R2-D2 encountered Jost Ellon (in the foreground) at the ruined dwelling of Lott Kemp.]] Around [[15 BBY/Legends|15 BBY]],{{SW|url=news/the-droids-re-animated-part-1|series=The Droids Re-Animated|text=Part 1|nolive=1}} dates the events of [[Droids (1986) 1|''Droids'' (1986) 1]] to around fifteen [[Standard year/Legends|years]] prior to those of {{Film|IV}}, which corresponds to approximately [[15 BBY/Legends|15 BBY]] per ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''. the crystal merchant [[Lott Kemp]] of Dodz arranged for the acquisition through the [[Intergalactic Droid Agency]] of two [[Service droid/Legends|servant droids]], C-3PO and the [[R2-series astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]] [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]]. Kugg subsequently sent the Destroyer to Kemp's [[Dwelling of Lott Kemp|dwelling]], which it destroyed, although the merchant himself escaped and fled the planet. Meanwhile, C-3PO and R2-D2, who were unaware of Kemp's plight, were traveling to Dodz aboard an [[Intergalactic Droid Agency shuttle]]. Upon the [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] mentioning to his companion their imminent arrival at their destination, two of the other droids transported by the [[Shuttle/Legends|starship]], who were bound for the [[Sulphur Pits of Vulcar]] on the harsh [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]] world [[Vulcar]],''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' picked up on C-3PO's namedrop of Dodz and mocked that destination as being backwater, with [[Unidentified droid 1|one]] of them quipping about the planet's diminutive size leading to it only possessing "half a [[Moon/Legends|moon]]," and the [[Unidentified droid 2|other droid]] commenting on the undesirable qualities of Dodz's barren surface. [[File:LandspeederRide-SWDroids1986-1.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Jost Ellon took C-3PO and R2-D2 on a landspeeder ride to his cave.]] Soon after disembarking, C-3PO and R2-D2 traveled to the address indicated for Kemp's dwelling, only to find it a still-smoldering ruin. There, they were confronted by Ellon, who was initially wary of the possibility of the droids working for Kugg. After letting them know of their owner's fate, Ellon offered the mechanical pair work helping him with salvaging and took them on a [[Jost Ellon's landspeeder|landspeeder]] ride to his cave abode. There, C-3PO informed Ellon about the X-1 he had found—with the youth expressing his disappointment over the Intergalactic Law Agency's termination of the droid series potentially depriving the population of Dodz of a defense against Kugg—leading to the trio activating the droid, which only had low amounts of energy available due to [[Power cell/Legends|power cell]] corrosion. ===Showdown=== {{Quote|Then the Citizen's Council of ''Dodz'' agrees! From now on, we give Kugg ''nothing''!|Althon|}} [[File:AlthonKugg.png|thumb|right|300px|The Citizen's Council of Dodz unsuccessfully attempted to defy Governor Kugg.]] Ellon and his three droids then ventured to attend the [[Standard month/Legends|monthly]] meeting of the [[Citizen's Council of Dodz]]. At the meeting, the councilors, including [[Head Councilman]] [[Althon]], expressed their intent to deny Kugg's demands; however, at that moment, the governor also arrived. With the Destroyer beginning to demolish the [[Council Dwelling]], Kugg coerced the council into not only agreeing to his latest tax increase, but to also pay triple the amount of [[Currency/Legends|money]] due. After Ellon and his companions arrived late to the scene, Althon, who was shocked at the youth's discovery of the X-1, attempted to talk him out of using the supposedly dangerous automaton. An old local [[Gender/Legends|man]], however, encouraged the scavenger to keep fighting, reminding him of the previous struggles of the inhabitants of Dodz. [[File:RobotFight-SWDroids1986-1.jpg|thumb|left|300px|The Destroyer fought the Ranger X-1 near Governor Kugg's mansion.]] On the following [[Local day/Legends|day]], as the councilors met with Kugg at his [[Mansion of Governor Kugg|mansion]] to hand over the latest tax payment, Ellon, who was surreptitiously observing the meeting, revealed his and the X-1's presence to the governor. Kugg ordered the Destroyer to attack the other droid, leading to a brawl between the two mechanical combatants. When the fight began to favor the Destroyer due to the X-1's lower energy levels, R2-D2 tapped into the power systems of Kugg's droid and transferred power to the X-1, allowing the latter to then use all of his energy reserves to disintegrate the Destroyer. The councilors subsequently restrained Kugg, intending to hand him over to an [[intergalactic trial]]. Ellon also accepted Althon's apology and gratitude, only asking that the planet's population remember the role the X-1—who had become a motionless standing figure—had played in the liberation of Dodz. A short time later, an Intergalactic Droid Agency shuttle returned to the planet to pick up C-3PO and R2-D2 due to the latter having sustained critical [[microsynapse]] damage during the battle between the Destroyer and the X-1 and there not being adequate facilities on Dodz to effect the necessary repairs. ==Inhabitants== {{Quote|… At least Dodz is ''inhabited''!|C-3PO|}} Dodz was home to a population that included [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]]–speaking Humans. It had a profitable crystal mining industry and was governed by the Citizen's Council of Dodz, led by a Head Councilman. The young orphaned Jost Ellon made a meager living on Dodz by fixing and selling salvaged technology. ==Locations== {{Quote|Well, this is it! Not much… but it's ''home''!|Jost Ellon, welcoming C-3PO and R2-D2 to his cave|}} Dodz was the site of a number of buildings, including the Council Dwelling of the Citizen's Council of Dodz; the mansion of the planet's Governor Kugg, and the dwelling of the crystal merchant Lott Kemp. The crystal mines and the cave where Jost Ellon lived where also located there. After the battle between the Destroyer and the Ranger X-1 near Kugg's mansion, the deactivated X-1 remained standing on a small hill in a heroic pose. ==Behind the scenes== {{Quote|Now we've done it, ''R2-D2''! Those ''metal monsters'' may destroy this ''entire planet''! And ''us'' along with it!|C-3PO, on the battle between the Destroyer and the Ranger X-1 on the cover of ''Droids'' (1986) 1|}} Dodz first appeared in the [[Droids (1986) 1|first issue]] of [[Star Comics]]' [[Star Wars: Droids (1986)|''Star Wars: Droids'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series, which was written by [[David Manak]], penciled by [[John Romita Sr.]], and published in [[February]] [[1986]].''[[Marvel Age]]'' #36 The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Dodz [[Star system/Legends|system]], and thereby the planet itself, in grid square U-13. ==Appearances== {{Indexpage}} {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Dodz}} *''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic: Shadow of Revan]]'' {{Mo}} *[[Droids (1986) 1|''Droids'' (1986) 1]] {{1st}} *[[Droids (1986) 2|''Droids'' (1986) 2]] {{Mo}} ==Sources== *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *{{SW|url=news/the-droids-re-animated-part-1|series=The Droids Re-Animated|text=Part 1|nolive=1}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |nl=Dodz }} [[Category:Mining planets]] [[Category:Planets in the Outer Rim Territories]] [[Category:Planets in the Slice]] [[Category:Rocky planets]] [[Category:Zoraster sector locations]] {{RelatedCategories |[[Category:Dodz locations]] |[[Category:Dodz residents]] }}
